scope_note: Cert [468]: QA Witt Tower MI Ceiling Theory. Claim: The closed-form formula MI_ratio(q,r) = MI(q,r)/H(binary,q) for the T0/T1/T2 equal-thirds rank partition predicts all 6 binary domain MI_ratios from cert [467] within 2% (max delta=1.1% for SEP). The binary monotone law (MI_ratio strictly increases with q for fixed r) is proven numerically over a dense grid (0 violations / 320 pairs). For r>=1/3, MI_ratio is strictly increasing in r (0 violations / 264 pairs). The ~70% convergence between ENSO (observed 0.699) and SEP (observed 0.697) is explained: both fall in the [0.68, 0.72] zone of the formula surface, despite physically unrelated mechanisms. The formula derives purely from the rank-uniform partition assumption (Theorem NT: all domain signals are observer projections; only integer tier labels enter the QA layer). MAPPING: observer projection (float signal) -> rank -> bin=floor(rank*27/N) in Z/27Z -> tier T=bin//9 in {T0,T1,T2} -> MI with event label. CERTIFIED: (C1) 6 binary domains within 2% PASS. (C2) binary monotone q-direction 0 violations PASS. (C3) SEP+ENSO in [0.68,0.72] zone PASS. (C4) perfect info limit MI_ratio->1 PASS. (C5) monotone in r (r>=1/3) 0 violations PASS. (C6) ranking preserved PASS. 6/6 PASS. PRIMARY SOURCES: Shannon CE (1948) doi:10.1002/j.1538-7305.1948.tb01338.x; Wall HS (1960) doi:10.1080/00029890.1960.11989541; cert [467] (empirical inputs, commit 4157fee2). Structural parents: cert [110] (Witt tower), cert [467] (cross-domain MI survey). Validated 2026-06-19.
